overview: Grixis Midrange in Modern leverages a blend of instant-speed interaction, efficient removal, and resilient creatures to control the pace of the game. By utilizing Orcish Bowmasters and Counterspell early, the deck disrupts aggressive strategies while Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides the card advantage needed to outlast opponents. The goal is to stabilize against early pressure, trade efficiently, and close out games with superior resource management.
win_conditions: Outlasting opponents through Tamiyo's card advantage engine
Closing games with efficient removal and combat disruption
Securing board control by countering key threats and winning trades

keep_criteria: Hands featuring Counterspell or Orcish Bowmasters with playable lands
Mana bases that ensure a smooth turn 3 or 4 for Tamiyo
Hands with a clear path to early interaction and a follow-up threat
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (preferably duals or fetches for fixing), a turn-1 or turn-2 interaction like Counterspell or removal, and a low-cost threat or card draw.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking any instant-speed interaction or removal
Mana screws with four or more lands or zero lands
Hands that cannot stabilize against early aggressive pressure
Mulligan if you have 0-1 lands, all high-cost spells, or no interaction in the first two turns.

early_game: Play lands and hold up mana for Counterspell to answer early threats
Deploy Orcish Bowmasters to bounce aggressive creatures and disrupt opponent's rhythm
Use Tamiyo to dig for answers or draw into removal when the board is manageable
Turn 1: Play a land and cast a cheap interaction or set up mana if possible.
Turn 2: Develop your mana base, cast a 2-drop threat or value engine, and prioritize untapping for color fixing.
If facing aggro or ramp, hold interaction for turn 2-3 threats rather than overcommitting early.
mid_game: Stabilize against early pressure by trading efficiently while maintaining card advantage
Set up board presence with resilient creatures that can withstand removal
Sequence countermagic to protect key threats and force unfavorable trades
Preserve mana for interaction until threats appear, and use card draw to maintain hand size without overextending.
Manage your graveyard by using bounce or exile effects when recursion becomes a threat, and avoid tapping out for non-essential spells.
Commit threats only when you have a clear path to resolution or sufficient answers to protect them, holding back high-value permanents if the board is unstable.
Prioritize trading efficiently and never overcommit to a board state you cannot recover from.
late_game: Leverage Tamiyo's draw engine to out-resource opponents and find finishers
Use countermagic to protect your board state and answer late-game win conditions
Close out games with incremental value, combat tricks, and superior card selection
High-impact planeswalkers like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student, and evasive threats win the game by establishing an unanswerable board state.
Close out games by leveraging card advantage to out-value opponents and forcing them to spend resources on multiple threats.
Go all-in when you have card advantage, board control, and the opponent is low on resources or locked out of their game plan.
Commit when you can safely resolve a win condition or planeswalker without fearing a sweep.

key_cards.name: Orcish Bowmasters
key_cards.notes: Use to bounce aggressive threats or force unfavorable trades, buying time for your game plan.

key_cards.name: Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student
key_cards.notes: Activate to draw into removal or countermagic, and use its death trigger to stabilize against board wipes.

key_cards.name: Counterspell
key_cards.notes: Hold up to protect key threats, answer opponent's win conditions, or secure board control during combat.

sequencing_tips: Hold Counterspell for opponent's most impactful spell rather than using it preemptively
Play Orcish Bowmasters before committing resources to the board to maximize tempo
Activate Tamiyo after opponent passes to maximize information and minimize counter risk
A common mistake is overcommitting to the board without answers, so always keep at least one interaction in hand for turn 3-4.
Protect Tamiyo at all costs, as her loyalty abilities provide essential card advantage and board control.
Be mindful of Commander tax and use it to your advantage by casting spells in response to triggers or when opponents are tapped out.

matchups.advice: Prioritize early Counterspell and Orcish Bowmasters to slow the clock, then stabilize with Tamiyo.
Play around their interaction by sequencing threats carefully and using Tamiyo to dig for answers.
Save Counterspell for their key combo pieces and use Orcish Bowmasters to disrupt their setup.

Use cheap, uncounterable threats, split your spells across turns, or use protection and enablers to bypass counters. Overload counters with multiple low-cost spells or time your key plays to resolve after they've spent their interaction.

Prioritize early interaction, artifact hate, and board wipes to prevent opponents from establishing a massive mana base. Answer key ramp pieces immediately and avoid trading into their early threats without clearing the board.

Win the resource war by prioritizing card draw, targeted removal, and protection over generic sweepers. Out-value them by resolving more threats, answering their key pieces first, and leveraging Tamiyo's card draw to maintain hand superiority.

common_threats: Watch for combo pieces like Storm counts, Titan triggers, or Blink targets, and note when opponents tap out for big spells.
Track graveyard accumulation for recursion threats and monitor when key interaction is on the stack to time your plays.
